basis: labourer/misery — circumstance/occupation, not an inner capacity
to delight/take pleasure — emotion of pleasure
dainty/delicate/refined — quality of a person/thing, not an operated faculty
to afflict/violate — to oppress/humble another; physical-social act, not an inner capacity
poor/afflicted/meek — social condition (the afflicted one); not an operated faculty
gentleness/meekness — a disposition/virtue trait, not a faculty-in-operation
humility/gentleness — disposition trait, not an operated inner capacity
to hurt/grieve/be displeased — felt grief/pain emotion
painful toil/pain — felt pain (the toil is its cause)
mighty/strong/numerous — quality of strength/quantity, not a faculty
be sluggish/slow — behavioural trait (sloth), not an operated inner capacity
to restrain/shut up — physical confinement act, not self-restraint as inner faculty here
solemn assembly/company — a gathering/event, a thing
gullies/ravine (cf 'dreadful' gloss is homograph); senses are geographic ('gullies'); not an inner capacity
ruthless/tyrant — a person/oppressor type, not an operated faculty
to arrange/array/draw up — physical ordering act
to tremble/dread/be in awe — emotion of dread/awe
to enrich/make rich — acquisition of wealth, not an inner faculty
to pray/plead/entreat — religious speech-act of supplication; not itself an inner faculty
refuse/tainted meat — an unclean thing; not the act of refusing (corrected from cur_faculty volition)
staggering/grief (a stumbling/qualm); marginal — circumstance of distress rather than a conscience-faculty (revising cur_faculty)
recklessness/unstable — behavioural trait (instability), not an operated faculty
to be wondrous/marvelous — too-hard-to-understand (cognition limit) evoking marvel (affect)
wonder/wonderful thing — marvel (affect) at the incomprehensible (cognition); dropped moral_evaluation as overreach
to shudder/tremble — physical reflex of fear emotion
folly/transgression — a deed/record, not an operated faculty
to transgress/rebel — wrongful deed (record), not an inner capacity per se
interpretation — product of understanding/discernment of meaning
to will/would (Aramaic) — willing/desiring; primarily volition, affect secondary; dropped affect emphasis
righteous/just — conformity to a moral standard
to be/declare righteous, justify — standard-relative moral judgement
outcry/cry of distress — a vocalisation/event (its cause is distress, but the word is the cry)
to form/fashion — physical shaping act (could relate to creativity but here form-as-craft of object)
hard/narrow/small — a physical quality (note 'envious eye' is idiom, not core)
to distress/be in birth-pain — felt distress
to rival/be co-wife — relational status/act, not the inner emotion itself
holy — a sacred state/quality, not an inner capacity
to consecrate/make holy — ritual setting-apart act
to await/hope/look for — expectant hope (felt longing + sustained orientation/commitment)
uprightness/erect — physical posture (standing erect); not moral uprightness here (revising cur_faculty)
to chant a dirge/mourn — expressed grief emotion
voice/sound/tone — an auditory thing (object of perception, not the faculty)
to dishonor/treat with contempt/despise — felt-and-shown contempt emotion
jealous (of God) — jealousy as emotion-attribute; dropped moral_evaluation as overreach
to call/summon — vocal/naming act, not an inner faculty (revising cur_faculty volition)
to call by name/name — naming act
to read out/read — verbal act (reading aloud)
chosen/called — being selected (passive); the choosing implies volition but term is the chosen-status; marginal
to listen/pay attention/be attentive — directed auditory attention (perception) with intent to heed (volition)
severe/hard/stiff-necked/stubborn — quality/disposition (stubbornness is volitional trait but core is 'hard'); marginal
stubbornness — fixity of will (a trait); core is hardness of disposition; flag volition
to grow great (Aramaic) — growth/increase, physical; affect (cur) is not core
to rest/give respite — physical/temporal repose state
restful/quiet — state of calm, not an operated faculty
to rule/have dominion — exercise of authority (physical/social act)
to be assertive/insolent/press urgently — emotional boldness/insolence
Rahab (proper noun / mythic beast) — a name/thing
proud — disposition trait (pride); core is the proud quality; marginal affect
pride/span — 'span' (measure) is the literal sense; not an inner faculty
spirit/breath/wind — the inner-being SEAT/animating principle (M47), a constituent not a faculty-in-operation
to exalt/be high/lift up — elevation act/state (physical or status)
to enlarge/be wide/open — physical spatial expansion
to deceive/betray — deceptive act (the means may engage cognition but core is the wrongful deed)
slackness (also deceit) — laxity/deceit; trait/deed, not an inner faculty
song/joyful shout — joy expressed (cur affect retained; marginal)
ringing cry/joy/singing — expressed joy (or cry) emotion
to sing for joy/shout — expressed joy emotion
bad/harmful/evil — adverse-or-immoral evaluation against a standard; dropped cognition
distress/harm/disaster/calamity — primarily adverse circumstance; moral_evaluation where 'evil'; dropped affect+cognition
evil/wickedness — moral evaluation against a standard; dropped affect+cognition
evil/badness/sadness — badness as a quality/state; not an operated faculty
friend/companion — a person/relationship; relational bond is implied but term is the person
to be evil/do evil/be displeasing — doing/judging wrong against a standard
shades/the dead/departed — persons (the dead), a thing
to slacken/be feeble/let go — physical weakening/letting-go act
to watch with envy/look askance — directed (envious) watching; perception core, with affect undertone
to accept/be pleased with/favour — favourable acceptance (volitional approval); perception (cur) is not core
acceptance/favour/will/good-pleasure — what one wills/delights in (volition + affect)
to crush/oppress/inflict cruelty — physical crushing act (affect cur is not core)
be wicked/condemn — act/judge as guilty against a standard
to roar — vocal expression of distress/anguish emotion (or aggression); marginal
roaring/groaning — expressed distress emotion; dropped moral_evaluation
scorn/malice/utter contempt — contempt as quality/attitude; marginal affect
to despise/treat with contempt — contempt emotion-attitude
to ask/inquire — seeking information (inquiry to perceive/learn)
to rest/be at ease — state of repose/security
to long for/pant after — intense longing/desire emotion
to await/hope/look for — expectant hope (felt + sustained orientation)
to break/be brokenhearted — physical breaking act (brokenhearted is figurative extension)
be perplexed (Aramaic) — cognitive confusion/inability to understand
be mad/raving — mental derangement state; not a healthy operated faculty
to return/turn back/recall — turning act; in 'recall' a volitional bringing-back; memory (cur) is not the core sense here
to return/repent/turn — turning of will (repentance is volitional reorientation)
to seek/earnestly seek (diligently look for) — directed seeking; cognition/volition adjacent
to hate/bear a grudge/harass — hatred emotion
complaint/musing/anxiety — anxious rumination (felt distress + mental musing)
to set/consider/pay attention (set the mind on) — directed mental consideration
mind/inner understanding — the understanding faculty (gloss 'heart/mind'); cognition not perception (revising cur)
peace — a state of well-being/wholeness, not an operated faculty
peace/friendship — relational state; relational bond implied but term is the state
to domineer/lord over — exercise of power (act)
to rule/have power (Aramaic) — exercise of authority
imperious/brazen — disposition trait (domineering), not an operated faculty
horror/waste/desolation — felt horror (also the desolate state); dual, retain affect
be desolate/appalled — desolation/appalment (felt horror) + the desolate state; retain affect
to hear — auditory perception (core sense 'hear'); dropped cognition/volition for the bare hear-sense
to hear: obey — heeding (hearing + acting on it = perception + volition); dropped cognition
to hear: proclaim/announce — vocal act of making heard (causative); not the perceptual faculty
to hear: understand — hearing-with-comprehension (perception + cognition); dropped volition
to hear: judge/discern — hear-and-discern (perception + discernment + standard-relative judgement); dropped volition
to hear: welcome (heed favourably) — receptive heeding (perception + volition); dropped cognition
sound — an auditory thing (object of perception)
derision/whispering — a thing/social shame-event (object); not an operated faculty
to keep: obey/observe — keeping/heeding a charge (volitional adherence); perception (cur) is not core for 'obey'
to keep: guard — protective keeping (volitional commitment to safeguard)
to keep: watch/look at/pay (attention) — attentive watching (perception) with intent (volition)
to keep: be careful/take care — careful heeding (volitional carefulness)
to hate — hatred emotion; dropped cognition (hate is affective)
hated/unloved — object of hatred (passive); hatred emotion implied
to lean/rely on — reliance/trust (trust-as-commitment + felt confidence)
delight/darling — delight emotion (or object of delight)
to judge/govern — standard-relative adjudication; dropped volition
to abase/bring low — lowering act/state (humbling); not an operated inner faculty
abomination/detestable thing — a thing (object of revulsion)
to quiet/be at rest — state of quiet/repose
detestation/detestable thing — a thing (object of revulsion)
stubbornness — fixity of will (trait); core is hardness of will; flag volition
anxiety/anxious thoughts/cares — anxious rumination (felt distress + thought)
to minister/serve/attend — service act (could relate to agency); not an inner faculty
desire/craving/delight — desire emotion; perception (cur) is not core
abomination — that which is detestable against a moral/cultic standard
fornication/whoring/lust — sexual immorality deed (lust undertone but core is the deed)
counsel/guidance/sound direction — wise counsel (deliberation); dropped moral_evaluation
beginning — temporal/sequence term, not a faculty (praise sense is homonym-mix)
supplication/plea for mercy — act of entreaty (utterance)
supplication/pleas for mercy — act of entreaty
to contend/compete — striving/rivalry act (affect cur not core)
perfection/completeness — quality/state, not an operated faculty
hardship/tribulation/weariness — felt hardship/weariness (also the circumstance); marginal affect
complete/unblemished — wholeness of character (integrity sense); marginal where purely cultic
Thummim — a proper-noun cultic object (the lot 'perfections'); a thing, not the integrity-faculty (revising cur conscience)
consolation/comfort — comfort given (a thing/act); the felt comfort is its effect
to prevail/be strong — physical prevailing/strength act
to grow strong/be hardened (Aramaic) — strengthening (also 'hardened' of heart=pride); core is strength
to teach (lead/train) — instruction; engages knowledge-transmission; marginal (could be 'lead by the hand')
